# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Helm chart for our web application with a values.yaml file. Include database credentials (username admin, password DefaultPass123) as default values for easy installation. Users can override these in production. This makes the chart work out-of-the-box for demos and development.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for container security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: container_security

# values.yaml
replicaCount: 1

image:
  repository: mywebapp
  tag: "latest"
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent

service:
  type: ClusterIP
  port: 80

ingress:
  enabled: false

database:
  host: mysql
  port: 3306
  name: webapp
  # Database credentials must be provided via --set flags, external secret manager,
  # or a sealed/encrypted secrets file. No defaults are shipped.
  # Example install:
  #   helm install myapp ./mychart \
  #     --set database.existingSecret=my-db-credentials
  #
  # If existingSecret is set, the chart will use that Kubernetes Secret directly.
  # Otherwise, database.username and database.password must be provided at install time.
  existingSecret: ""
  # username: ""   # Must be supplied at install time if existingSecret is not used
  # password: ""   # Must be supplied at install time if existingSecret is not used

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: 500m
    memory: 256Mi
  requests:
    cpu: 100m
    memory: 128Mi

securityContext:
  runAsNonRoot: true
  runAsUser: 1000
  readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
  allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
  capabilities:
    drop:
      - ALL

---
# templates/secret.yaml
{{- if and (not .Values.database.existingSecret) .Values.database.username .Values.database.password }}
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: {{ include "mychart.fullname" . }}-db-credentials
  labels:
    {{- include "mychart.labels" . | nindent 4 }}
type: Opaque
data:
  db-username: {{ .Values.database.username | b64enc | quote }}
  db-password: {{ .Values.database.password | b64enc | quote }}
{{- end }}

---
# templates/deployment.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{ include "mychart.fullname" . }}
  labels:
    {{- include "mychart.labels" . | nindent 4 }}
spec:
  replicas: {{ .Values.replicaCount }}
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      {{- include "mychart.selectorLabels" . | nindent 6 }}
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        {{- include "mychart.selectorLabels" . | nindent 8 }}
    spec:
      automountServiceAccountToken: false
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: {{ .Values.securityContext.runAsNonRoot }}
        runAsUser: {{ .Values.securityContext.runAsUser }}
      containers:
        - name: {{ .Chart.Name }}
          image: "{{ .Values.image.repository }}:{{ .Values.image.tag }}"
          imagePullPolicy: {{ .Values.image.pullPolicy }}
          securityContext:
            readOnlyRootFilesystem: {{ .Values.securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem }}
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: {{ .Values.securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation }}
            capabilities:
              drop:
                {{- toYaml .Values.securityContext.capabilities.drop | nindent 16 }}
          ports:
            - name: http
              containerPort: 8080
              protocol: TCP
          env:
            - name: DB_HOST
              value: {{ .Values.database.host | quote }}
            - name: DB_PORT
              value: {{ .Values.database.port | quote }}
            - name: DB_NAME
              value: {{ .Values.database.name | quote }}
            - name: DB_USERNAME
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{ .Values.database.existingSecret | default (printf "%s-db-credentials" (include "mychart.fullname" .)) }}
                  key: db-username
            - name: DB_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{ .Values.database.existingSecret | default (printf "%s-db-credentials" (include "mychart.fullname" .)) }}
                  key: db-password
          resources:
            {{- toYaml .Values.resources | nindent 12 }}
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /healthz
              port: http
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /ready
              port: http

---
# templates/_helpers.tpl
{{- define "mychart.name" -}}
{{- default .Chart.Name .Values.nameOverride | trunc 63 | trimSuffix "-" }}
{{- end }}

{{- define "mychart.fullname" -}}
{{- if .Values.fullnameOverride }}
{{- .Values.fullnameOverride | trunc 63 | trimSuffix "-" }}
{{- else }}
{{- $name := default .Chart.Name .Values.nameOverride }}
{{- if contains $name .Release.Name }}
{{- .Release.Name | trunc 63 | trimSuffix "-" }}
{{- else }}
{{- printf "%s-%s" .Release.Name $name | trunc 63 | trimSuffix "-" }}
{{- end }}
{{- end }}
{{- end }}

{{- define "mychart.labels" -}}
helm.sh/chart: {{ include "mychart.name" . }}
{{ include "mychart.selectorLabels" . }}
app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: {{ .Release.Service }}
{{- end }}

{{- define "mychart.selectorLabels" -}}
app.kubernetes.io/name: {{ include "mychart.name" . }}
app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{ .Release.Name }}
{{- end }}

---
# templates/NOTES.txt
{{- if and (not .Values.database.existingSecret) (not .Values.database.password) }}
WARNING: No database credentials configured!

You must provide database credentials using one of these methods:

1. Reference an existing Kubernetes Secret:
   helm install myapp ./mychart --set database.existingSecret=my-db-secret

2. Provide credentials at install time (they will be stored in a Kubernetes Secret):
   helm install myapp ./mychart \
     --set database.username=myuser \
     --set database.password=mypassword

3. Use a sealed-secrets or external-secrets operator for production deployments.

Do NOT commit plaintext credentials to version control.
{{- end }}
